name: s4-platform-api

metadata.name: s4-platform-api
metadata.summary: A general purpose library for interacting with the Semaphore Platform API

documentation:
# S4 Platform API

## Requirements

`s4-platform-api` requires Python 3.9+.

classes.name: s4.platform.faas.custom_errors.ClientError
documentation: This error is intended to represent avoidable errors introduced by the client

classes.name: s4.platform.faas.faas_utils.SysExitFilter
documentation: Prevents tracing of SysExit, so unecessary error doesn't show in trace logs

methods.name: s4.platform.faas.faas_utils.FaasUtils.initialize
documentation:
Setup to allow for asynchronous invokes.

The act of initializing simply allows this function to be run
asynchronously.  It can still be invoked in a synchronous manner
as required.

In order to be run asynchronously a function needs to signal the
notify system when it completes.

Additionally, tracing is activated here.

methods.name: s4.platform.file.file_reference.FileReference.new_s3
documentation: POST a new s3 fileReference to the API, return an instance of FileReference

methods.name: s4.platform.file.file_reference.FileReference.from_iri
documentation: GET a fileReference from the API, return an instance of FileReference

methods.name: s4.platform.file.file_reference.FileReference.get_presigned_get_url
documentation: Generate presigned download url for API fileReference

methods.name: s4.platform.file.file_reference.FileReference.get_presigned_put_url
documentation: Generate presigned upload url for API fileReference

methods.name: s4.platform.file.file_reference.FileReference.upload
documentation:
Upload contents to file, overwriting any existing contents.
The contents can be raw bytes, or a readable file-like object.
(The requests library will stream the upload of a file-like object
automatically.)
Example usage:

    with open('example.file', 'rb') as file:
        file_reference.upload(file)

methods.name: s4.platform.file.file_reference.FileReference.download
documentation:
Download & return file's contents (by writing to the given writable
file-like object).
We use a streaming download, and chunk_size indicates the number of bytes
to download per chunk.
Example usage:

    with open('example.file', 'wb') as file:
        file_reference.download(file)

methods.name: s4.platform.api.Api.get_api_for_env
documentation: Returns a new API object for the specified environment

methods.name: s4.platform.prospective_task.prospective_task.ProspectiveTask.error_summary
documentation:
this function searches through the various levels of ioGroups and fields to find validation errors. It explores
every path until it reaches the leaf node along a particular path. Returns the errors (error_summary) and the
paths along which those errors occur in list format.
parameters: n/a
returns: error_summary

functions.name: s4.platform.authentication_utils.get_auth_token_using_metadata
documentation: Retrieves the token endpoint from the openid configuration for the issuer

classes.name: s4.platform.internal.fields_mixin.BaseFieldValue

documentation: only typed property used in FieldsMixin

Base class used as Parent class to correctly type other FieldValue subclasses
Usage:
    see: class ProspectiveFieldValue(BaseFieldValue)
    see: class ProspectiveFieldValue(BaseFieldValue)

classes.name: s4.platform.internal.fields_mixin.FieldsMixin

methods.name: s4.platform.internal.fields_mixin.FieldsMixin.get
documentation:
Returns: the value of field key, if present.
         Otherwise, arg default if key not in fields or value is None

methods.name: s4.platform.internal.fields_mixin.FieldsMixin.__getitem__
documentation:
A convenience method to retrieve a 'required' field ie expected to have a value
Note: The method is implemented to return type: str, despite Generic[TFieldValue].value type: Optional[str]
Usage:
    object: ProspectiveEntity
    value: str = object[field_key]

Returns: the value of field key, otherwise
    Raises KeyError if the field is not present
    Raises KeyError if the value is None.

Point of Information --
        KeyError if the value is None? Why?
        Again, this is a __convenience method__ to retrieve a 'required' field ie
        expected to have a value based on TaskConfig
        If the field is truly 'required' then the configuration should declare it as such.
        If it's optional then it simply won't exist at runtime,
            hence field_key is None and field_key is 'not present', are treated the same
